name: ABAP MCP Server
what_it_does: ABAP MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with SAP ABAP development environments — accessing ABAP class and function module definitions, reading SAP system metadata, querying ABAP development artifacts, and integrating AI assistance into SAP ABAP development workflows. Enables AI coding assistants to understand SAP system context for more accurate ABAP code generation.
use_cases: Reading ABAP class and function module definitions from SAP systems from AI coding agents
Querying SAP system metadata and domain objects from development agents
Providing SAP system context to AI code generators from ABAP development agents
Navigating ABAP development artifacts (programs, classes, interfaces) from research agents
SAP-aware AI assistance for ABAP development from developer productivity agents
not_for: Teams not doing SAP ABAP development (use other language MCPs for non-SAP development)
Direct execution of ABAP code (this provides read access to metadata, not code execution)
Non-technical SAP users without ABAP development access
best_when: An SAP ABAP developer needs AI assistance with SAP-specific code — providing system context to AI coding tools so they can generate accurate ABAP that matches the system's existing structures.
avoid_when: You're not doing SAP development. ABAP is a niche enterprise language specific to SAP ERP systems.

auth.notes: SAP system credentials required. Configure SAP backend URL, username/password or OAuth2. ABAP Development Tools (ADT) REST API used. Enterprise SAP credentials with development access needed.

pricing.free_tier_limits: SAP ABAP requires SAP license (enterprise software)
pricing.paid_tiers: SAP license costs are enterprise (significant cost); MCP server is free
pricing.notes: SAP licenses are enterprise-priced. MCP server is free open source from marianfoo (SAP community contributor). SAP BTP trial available for testing.

performance.rate_limits: SAP ADT API rate limits; enterprise SAP systems typically have generous limits

known_agent_gotchas: SAP system configuration is enterprise-specific — no universal setup; varies by SAP version and config
ABAP ADT API may differ between SAP S/4HANA versions — verify compatibility
SAP credentials have significant enterprise access — scope to development system only
Niche community MCP for SAP developers — by marianfoo, an active SAP community contributor
SAP system connectivity may require VPN for on-premise systems
ABAP concepts are unique to SAP — agents need SAP domain knowledge to be useful
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS. SAP credentials with enterprise access. SAP systems contain sensitive business data. Use development system credentials, not production. Enterprise credential management required.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS. SAP enterprise credentials. SAP systems contain sensitive business data. Use dev system only. VPN may be required for on-premise.
